paper_threads.summary: Introduces Hyperagents, an AI system that not only improves at solving tasks but also improves how it improves itself, building on the Darwin Gödel Machine concept.
Announces publication of The AI Scientist paper in Nature, showing fully automated AI research with scaling law results demonstrating improvement with more compute.
Shares research on large-scale RL in simulation without demos or reward engineering, solving dynamic, dexterous and contact-rich tasks with reactive behaviors.
Introduces Multi-Answer RL, a method to train language models to provide diverse answers rather than collapsing to single mode responses like always recommending Barcelona.
Presents PivotRL paper on high accuracy agentic post-training at low compute cost, one of the techniques used for Nemotron-3 post-training.
Releases MolmoWeb, built from scratch with Molmo2 for web agents, including all models and datasets for building SOTA web agents from open source VLMs.
Discusses scaling law bias in parabolic IsoFLOP fits used by major companies, potentially misallocating millions in compute at frontier scales.

announcements.summary: Announces Gemini 3.1 Flash Live as their highest quality audio & voice model with lower latency, better precision, and more natural interactions.
Launches OpenAI Safety Bug Bounty program focused on identifying AI abuse and safety risks across OpenAI products, building on their Security Bug Bounty.
Releases Cohere transcribe, a SOTA open source transcription model running in the browser, with weights available on Hugging Face.
Announces Cohere's new speech-to-text model that ranks #1 on Hugging Face Open ASR Leaderboard, setting new benchmark for transcription performance.
Releases slime v0.2.4 with focus on profiling and observability, including rollout trace timeline viewer and real-time W&B reporting.

discussions.summary: Observes that RL is the dominant AI paradigm but old school RL people are mostly uninterested and sitting out the LLM stage, contrary to expectations.
Notes Cursor trains one Composer 2 checkpoint every 5 hours, calling it interesting early signs of 'continual learning' in production.
Points out conversation paradox: people say LLMs are great for learning but when asked, admit there's no point since LLMs do better jobs already.
Discusses technical details of Cursor Composer 2, including scaling study of RL performance impact from continual pretraining and MTP head addition for speculative decoding.
Questions ARC-AGI-3's claim of being 'the only unsaturated agentic intelligence benchmark' by pointing out NetHack has been unsaturated since 2020.
